rationale: The US-Iran peace deal moving to formal signing on Friday is an incremental step from last cycle's confirmation — markets and oil responding positively; the Tren de Aragua leader killing is confirmed action but continuation of last cycle's development.

top_events.title: US, Iran to sign peace deal Friday; Hormuz reopening confirmed
top_events.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire moves to formal signing — an incremental step from last cycle's confirmation. Oil prices falling and markets soaring provide direct benefit to US consumers and investors.

top_events.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike
top_events.summary: Trump confirms a US air strike killed the Tren de Aragua gang leader — a confirmed military action, but continuation of the established domestic security operation from last cycle.

top_events.title: Stock markets soar, oil falls as US-Iran deal confirmed
top_events.summary: US financial markets are rising and oil prices falling on the Iran deal — directly beneficial to US consumers and investors, though this is a continuation of last cycle's market reaction.
